title: EU Blue Card for Highly Skilled Workers
summary: The EU Blue Card allows non-EU nationals with a university degree to work and live in Germany. It's the fastest route to permanent residence - you can apply for settlement permit after just 33 months (or 27 months with B1 German language skills).
steps: Secure a job offer meeting the minimum salary threshold (€45,552 in 2024, higher for shortage occupations)
Obtain an employment contract or binding job offer letter from your German employer
Apply for the EU Blue Card at the German embassy in your home country (before entering Germany), OR
If already in Germany legally, apply at your local Ausländerbehörde (Immigration Office)
Submit required documents: passport, employment contract, university degree certificate, proof of health insurance
Attend biometric appointment and provide fingerprints
Collect your EU Blue Card from Ausländerbehörde
documents: Valid passport (with at least 12 months validity)
University degree certificate (Anerkennung if from non-EU country) - 'Hochschulabschlusszeugnis'
Employment contract or binding job offer - 'Arbeitsvertrag'
Proof of health insurance (public or private) - 'Krankenversicherung'
Motivation letter explaining the job and qualifications
Application form (Antrag auf Erteilung eines Visums / Aufenthaltserlaubnis)
office: Ausländerbehörde (Foreigners' Registration Office) - locally known asAusländeramt
notes: Minimum salary for 2024 is €45,552. For STEM shortage occupations (IT, engineering, medicine), the threshold drops to €39,682. Your employer typically notifies the Bundesagentur für Arbeit (Federal Employment Agency) first. The Blue Card is tied to your employer initially - changing jobs requires notification but not a new visa.
